channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Faces Scrutiny Over Personal Security Detail for Girlfriend
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel reportedly reacted angrily to an internal suggestion that the extensive security detail assigned to his girlfriend, country singer Alexis Wilkins, should undergo an ethics revi...

item.title: Kenya must fully use teacher‑coaching scheme to raise education quality
item.description: The article talks about a recent coaching initiative in Elgeyo Marakwet County where 12 coaches trained in a French municipality have been deployed to wards to support close to 900 teachers. The write...

item.title: Lopez Family Dispute Shifts from Boardroom to Courtrooms, Ceasefire Limited
item.description: The Lopez family feud has taken a temporary pause with the withdrawal of the February 27 board resolution that sought to remove Federico “Piki” Lopez as president and CEO of Lopez Inc., the family's p...

item.title: Palestinian Ambassador Urges Unity During Nakba Day Commemoration
item.description: The Palestinian envoy to Tanzania has made a call for unity as communities prepare to mark Nakba Day, a day of remembrance for Palestinians displaced during the 1948 conflict. In a statement issued on...

item.title: Yanga Partners with GSM to Build 20,000-Seat Modern Stadium in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Dar es Salaam is set to witness a major boost in sports infrastructure with the announcement of a new 20,000-seater stadium for Young Africans (Yanga). GSM Group CEO, Aisha Mohamed, highlighted that t...

item.title: Man Testifies About Dog Attacks Leading to Boy's Death
item.description: In Edmonton, a man provided testimony in court regarding a violent dog attack incident that ultimately resulted in the death of a young boy. According to the man, two dogs attacked him simultaneously,...

item.title: Manipur: 28 Naga and Kuki hostages freed after fresh violence in Kangpokpi and Senapati districts
item.description: In the latest development from Manipur, the state police have confirmed that 28 out of approximately 38 persons from the Naga and Kuki communities, who were taken hostage by armed groups, have been re...

item.title: Decline in Commercial Crime Convictions Despite More Courts and Prosecutors
item.description: Over the past two decades, South Africa has seen a sharp decrease in convictions for commercial crimes even as reported cases have increased. The National Prosecuting Authority's Specialised Commercia...

item.title: Access to full news article unavailable; original story blocked
item.description: The news article from Herald Sun about police arresting teenagers after a third alleged arson attempt at Bar Bambi could not be accessed. The website displayed a message indicating that the content wa...

item.title: Tanzania prepares comprehensive tourism promotion for 2027 Afcon as co-host
item.description: Tanzania is set to co-host the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) together with Kenya and Uganda, and the government is using this big opportunity to boost the country's tourism sector. The Ministry o...

item.title: Suspect in Golders Green stabbings to stand trial for attempted murder next year
item.description: The news regarding the Golders Green stabbings centres around a suspect who is scheduled to face trial for attempted murder in the coming year. Unfortunately, the full article could not be accessed du...

item.title: Telangana HC denies interim relief to Union Minister's son in POCSO case
item.description: In Hyderabad, the Telangana High Court has refused to grant any interim protection from arrest to Bandi Bageerath, son of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, in a POCSO case filed against him. Justice ...

item.title: Elections Alberta investigates exposure of millions of voter records by separatist group
item.description: Court filings from the Court of King’s Bench have revealed more information about Elections Alberta’s investigation into a major voter privacy breach. The breach involved the Centurion Project, a regi...

item.title: Yutte Stensgaard and Her Iconic Roles in 1970s Hammer Horror Films
item.description: Born in 1946, Danish actress and model Yutte Stensgaard gained recognition in British cinema during the late 1960s and early 1970s. She became known for her Nordic beauty, long blonde hair, and captiv...

item.title: Government Approves Bill to Upgrade NEMC into Full Environmental Authority
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking steps to transform the National Environment Management Council (NEMC) into a fully-fledged authority. The proposed law has already been approved by the government an...

item.title: Suspect in Pangasinan Child Rape Case Apprehended in Manila
item.description: Authorities in Manila have arrested a suspect wanted for the alleged rape of a child with a disability in Pangasinan. The arrest was carried out by the Manila Police District in coordination with the ...

item.title: UK blocks entry to foreign far-right activists ahead of London rally
item.description: The UK government has prevented eleven foreign far-right activists from entering the country to attend a rally organised by anti-Islam campaigner Tommy Robinson. The event, titled Unite the Kingdom, i...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo results in 65 deaths
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has reported a new Ebola outbreak, which has already claimed the lives of 65 people. Health authorities are actively monitoring the situation and have deployed rapid r...

item.title: Zamfara launches first direct Hajj flight from Gusau International Airport
item.description: Zamfara State don mark big milestone as Gov Lawal flag off the first-ever Hajj flight from Gusau International Airport. Dis inaugural flight waka on Friday, carry total of 415 pilgrims, 288 men and 12...

item.title: Zara Tindall celebrates 45 and maintains key role within the Royal Family
item.description: Zara Tindall, daughter of Princess Anne, celebrates her 45th birthday recognised for her down-to-earth nature and popularity within the Royal Family. Often referred to as the family's 'secret weapon',...
